name: The First Bank Of Greenwich
description_short: Community-focused bank serving Connecticut and New York with personal and business banking, checking, savings, and digital services.
description_long: The First Bank of Greenwich is a regional community bank headquartered in Cos Cob, Connecticut, operating three physical branches across Connecticut and New York (Cos Cob, Stamford, and Port Chester). The bank emphasizes its deep roots in local communities and commitment to personalized service for both individual and small-to-mid-size business customers. Founded with a mission to provide 'hometown attitude and ethic,' the bank positions itself as a relationship-focused alternative to larger national institutions. The bank offers a comprehensive suite of retail and business banking products. On the personal side, they provide checking and savings accounts (including specialized products like Charitable Checking, Platinum Savings, and Pet Savings accounts), loan products, credit cards, and debit card options through Greenlight for financial literacy. Digital services include 24/7 online banking, mobile banking via their First Bank Mobile app, and remote deposit capabilities. Business customers can access business banking products, lines of credit, and the B2B Network referral program designed to strengthen local business connections. The First Bank of Greenwich distinguishes itself through explicit community involvement and social responsibility. They sponsor local cultural events (Avon Theatre, Bruce Museum), support financial literacy education in local schools, and offer charitable giving opportunities through their Charitable Checking product. Employees are described as community members who participate in local events. The B2B Network provides business customers with structured networking and referral opportunities beyond traditional banking services. As a community bank with only three branch locations, The First Bank of Greenwich is geographically limited to the Connecticut/New York border area and lacks the national reach or extensive product variety of larger institutions. While they offer modern digital services, their physical footprint and service area are significantly smaller than major banks. Customers outside their three branch locations would have limited in-person support options.
services: Savings accounts (Platinum Savings, Charitable Checking, Pet Savings)
Greenlight debit card and financial literacy app for minors
B2B Network (business referral and networking program)
pros: Offers specialized savings accounts including Charitable Checking (donates to local organizations), Pet Savings, and Platinum Savings with competitive rates
Provides financial literacy tools through Greenlight debit card and money app partnership for account holders' children
24/7 online and mobile banking with remote deposit capabilities for account management convenience
Active community involvement with documented support for local schools, arts organizations, and cultural events
B2B Network program specifically designed for business customers to facilitate referrals and networking
Three branch locations with extended Thursday hours (until 6pm) and Saturday service (9am-12pm)
Personal relationship-based service model with employees embedded in local community
cons: Only three physical branch locations (Cos Cob, Stamford, Port Chester) limiting accessibility for customers outside these areas
No mention of competitive interest rates, APYs, or fee structures on website to compare with larger institutions
Limited product information available online; customers must contact bank directly for specific account details and terms
No indication of FDIC insurance details, deposit insurance limits, or other regulatory protections explicitly stated
Website does not provide information about credit card APRs, rewards programs, or lending rate terms

contact.address: 444 E Putnam Ave, Cos Cob, CT 06807

best_for: Small-to-mid-size business owners in Connecticut and New York seeking relationship banking and local networking opportunities
Individuals in the Cos Cob, Stamford, or Port Chester areas wanting personalized service and community-focused banking
Customers prioritizing community impact and charitable giving through their banking relationship
Parents seeking financial literacy tools for children through Greenlight partnership
diagnosis: The First Bank of Greenwich is best for small business owners and individuals in the Connecticut/New York border region who value personalized community banking and want to support a locally-invested institution. The main caveat is geographic limitation—customers outside the three branch areas will have limited in-person support, and the bank's smaller size means fewer specialized products and services compared to national banks.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 444 E Putnam Ave, Cos Cob, CT 06807
